description_long: This is a medieval-style helmet made primarily of metal, featuring a smooth, rounded dome that covers the entire head. The front opening is wide and rectangular, allowing for clear visibility and breathability, while the lower edge extends downward to partially protect the cheeks. The helmet is adorned with a central raised ridge running from front to back, adding structural strength and a distinctive visual line. Small, circular rivets are visible around the sides and back, likely securing the inner lining or reinforcing the shell. A simple chin strap is attached for securing the helmet on the wearer's head, and the overall finish is a matte, slightly reflective gray metal with no decorative graphics.
description: A solid, rounded metal helmet with a wide front opening, central ridge, and visible rivets, finished in matte gray with a chin strap.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide rectangular opening, central ridge, and chin strap attachment.
description_view_1: Rear-side view highlights the smooth, rounded dome and multiple rivets along the back and sides.
description_view_2: Direct rear view displays the central ridge and symmetrical placement of rivets on the helmet's back.
description_view_3: Opposite rear-side view again shows the rounded dome and rivet pattern, with subtle surface texturing.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Six small, round ventilation holes are distributed symmetrically on the sides and back of the helmet.
